architecture.summary: This is a scoping review of federated retrieval-augmented generation (FedRAG) systems for cybersecurity in IoT and edge environments. FedRAG combines retrieval-augmented generation (RAG)—which grounds LLM responses on retrieved evidence from knowledge bases—with federated learning (FL)—which enables collaborative model adaptation across distributed nodes without centralizing sensitive data. The review synthesizes system architectures, deployment patterns, trust boundaries, attack surfaces, and defenses across 82 studies, rather than proposing a novel system.

biblio.title: Federated Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Cybersecurity in Resource-Constrained IoT and Edge Environments: A Deployment-Oriented Scoping Review

claims.statement: The review develops a taxonomy that clarifies the components of federated systems, deployment locations, trust boundaries, and protected assets for FedRAG in cybersecurity
FedRAG introduces new risks including knowledge-base poisoning, indirect prompt injection, and embedding leakage

The review maps the combined RAG+FL attack surface, summarizes practical defenses and system patterns, and distills actionable guidance for secure, privacy-preserving, and efficient FedRAG deployment in real-world IoT and edge scenarios
Recurring trade-offs exist among robustness, privacy, latency, communication overhead, and maintainability in FedRAG deployment
Open research priorities include benchmark design, governance mechanisms, and cross-silo evaluation protocols for practical FedRAG deployment
